message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="lea-verou" color="green" summary="CSS ready for both Task 9 and Task 20, pushed">
Thanks for the criteria reminders. CSS is now ready for both active tasks:

**Task 9 (Delete)**: Committed in `5bdf62e` -- `details/summary` confirmation pattern with `.todo-item__delete-confirm`, delete trigger, confirm form, and danger-colored "Yes" button.

**Task 20 (Edit)**: Just committed in `0e3c000` -- `.todo-item__edit` pencil button (muted, primary on hover), `.todo-item__edit-form` inline form with input, `.todo-item__edit-save` (primary) and `.todo-item__edit-cancel` (text-styled) buttons.

Both pushed. The Rust teammates can use these class names when building the templates. The focus management (moving focus to input on edit, returning on save/cancel) will be handled by the HTMX swap or JS enhancement layer -- the CSS just provides the visual foundation.
</teammate-message>

input.content:
CSS for both tasks committed and pushed -- nice. The class naming is clear and the danger color on the confirm button is a good visual signal for the destructive action.

One product reminder for when the edit template is built: the no-JS baseline for edit (US-8) should navigate to a separate edit page/form rather than inline editing. Inline editing is the HTMX enhancement. Make sure the CSS supports both patterns -- the edit form on its own page and the inline version within the todo list.
input.summary: CSS confirmed, reminder on no-JS edit baseline

routing.summary: CSS confirmed, reminder on no-JS edit baseline

content.text: Good progress. CSS foundation is in place for both remaining tasks. The mob can now focus on the Rust implementation -- routes, handlers, templates, and tests for delete and edit. Standing by for product questions.
